revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Futagozuka Kofun (Fukuyama)]][[ja:二子塚古墳 (福山市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Futagozuka Kofun | native_name =二子塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Futagozuka Kofun (Fukuyama), funkyu-5.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Futagozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Futagozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.55347222|N|133.31233333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama, Hiroshima|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late.6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|12.5|N|133|18|44.4|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Futagozuka Kofun'''|二子塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Nakashima Ekiyacho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama, Hiroshima|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}} in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2009.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140424|title=二子塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is noted for the large quantity of [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s discovered.
==Overview== The Futagozuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a low hill about 50 meters above sea level orientated west-southwest.The ancient {{ill|San'yōdō|en|San'yōdō|qid=Q1376722|lt=San'yōdō|1=San'yōdō}} highway passes through the southern foot of the tumulus, and it is located at a strategic point for east–west traffic The tumulus is 68 meters long, with a posterior circular diameter of 41 meters, anterior width of 30 meters, and six meters in height. It is surrounded by a moat ranging from 1.6 to four meters in width and 1.8 meters deep. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were present at the site. The tumulus contains [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s in both its anterior and posterior portions. The passage to the anterior burial chamber is 12.5 meters long, making it one of the largest in the prefecture. The sarcophagus is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stone quarried in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. From 2002 to 2005, the Fukuyama City Board of Education conducted an [[archaeological excavation]], finding that the sarcophagus had been damaged by grave robbers in antiquity and that the burial chamber had been considerably disturbed. Iron nails were found in the back of the burial chamber, suggesting that a wooden coffin was placed separately from the sarcophagus. Despite the robbery and lack of relic in situ, a considerable quantity of grave goods were still found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These include weapons such as a gilt-bronze double dragon ring pommel from an iron sword, iron spears, and iron arrowheads, horse gear such as saddle fittings, iron nails, and knives. The gilt-bronze twin dragon ring pommel has a design in which two dragons face each other and each holds a ball in its mouth, which is unique in Japan. As for earthenware, both {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} were excavated from various places in the chamber.The sue ware was identified as coming from kilns in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, and date the first burial in the burial chamber to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century. In addition, it is highly likely that additional burials were carried out in the early to mid 7th century, making this one of the final ''Kofun'' to be used in western Japan.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the {{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|qid=Q1053688|lt=Bizen|1=Bizen Province}} and {{ill|Bitchū Province|en|Bitchū Province|qid=Q1047514|lt=Bitchū Province|1=Bitchū Province}}s, huge keyhole-shaped Kofuns were built in the early and middleKofun period, but in {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}}, this Kofun suddenly appeared at a later date. The fact that the sarcophagus was made of Tatsuyama stone from Harima, which was used for keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], instead of the locally mined wave-shaped stone indicates a strong connection with the Yamato regime in the former [[Kingdom of Kibi]] around the 7th century.
The site is open to the public at all times, but is privately owned. It is located about eight minutes on foot from {{ill|Chikata Station|en|Chikata Station|qid=Q5097393|lt=Chikata Station|1=Chikata Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Fukuen Line|en|Fukuen Line|qid=Q899394|lt=Fukuen Line|1=Fukuen Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|qid=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Futagozuka Kofun (Fukuyama)}} *[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/196063.html Fukuyama City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuyama-kanko.com/travel/tourist/detail.php?id=48 Fukuyama Tourist Information]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120230.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
